commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The forecast items are cramped on smaller widths because there is no flex-basis or explicit width control for the items.
issues.fix: Add a minimum width to the forecast day stack items to ensure they distribute evenly or wrap cleanly.

issues.description: The component is purely presentational; adding a simple hover scale or shadow transition would enhance the 'card' feel.
issues.fix: Add a subtle transition effect on hover to the Card component.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses the design system tokens effectively, and adheres to the prop requirements. The layout is clean, although the forecast strip could benefit from fixed-width spacing to prevent text overlapping when labels vary in length.

evaluation.critique: The component has good data handling and clear hierarchy, but relies on hardcoded color fallbacks instead of purely using design tokens, and lacks interactive polish like hover states on forecast items. The layout is solid with proper spacing, though the forecast strip could use better visual grouping for individual day cards.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

as default};

issues.description: The grid elements are semantic buttons but lack ARIA labels describing their content, which is problematic for screen readers since they only display symbols.
issues.fix: Add aria-label={`Element ${el.name} (${el.symbol})`} to the button elements.

issues.description: The periodic table layout might overflow on smaller screens, and the grid cells lack specific alignment for the atomic number.
issues.fix: Ensure the container has a min-width to prevent layout collapse and use flexbox/grid inside the button to pin the atomic number to the top-left.

issues.description: The legend is hardcoded and doesn't dynamically map to the actual categories present in the data passed via props.
issues.fix: Derive the legend categories from the unique list of categories found in the elements prop.

evaluations.critique: The component effectively implements the required grid layout and filtering logic using clean React patterns. While it meets the core functional requirements, it needs improved accessibility labels for the grid buttons and a more dynamic approach to generating the legend.

evaluation.critique: The component effectively uses CSS Grid for the periodic table layout with proper design tokens throughout, but has some layout concerns: the 18-column grid may cause overflow on smaller screens (not truly responsive), and the grid gap uses a token while element padding/sizing use hardcoded pixel values. Polish is good with transitions and opacity filtering, though hover/focus states are missing from buttons and the legend category names are not user-friendly (e.g., 'alkali-metal' instead of 'Alkali Metal').

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Lack of visual indicators for weather conditions; relies solely on text/emoji which can look disconnected.
issues.fix: Consider adding a simple visual container or background effect based on the condition prop.

issues.description: No hover states on forecast items or the card itself.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover transition (scale or shadow lift) for the forecast items to indicate they are data points.

issues.description: The forecast emojis are rendered as text without ARIA labels.
issues.fix: Add an 'aria-label' to the emoji display (e.g., 'Sunny icon') so screen readers don't ignore or misinterpret the visual data.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and correctly utilizes the design system primitives. It effectively handles the data structure provided. To move to production-ready, it needs slightly more refined interactive states and better semantic labeling for the weather icons.

evaluation.critique: The component has good structure and properly uses props for all data with no hardcoding. However, it mixes design token variables with hardcoded fallback hex colors (violating token-only requirement), lacks interactive polish (no hover states), and uses inline styles instead of spacing tokens for the forecast grid flex layout.

commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The legend for categories is missing, which was explicitly requested in the prompt.
issues.fix: Add a dedicated section or sub-component that maps categories to their respective colors for user reference.

issues.description: The custom search input uses native HTML styling instead of the library's design system components, and the periodic table cells lack focus states and ARIA labels.
issues.fix: Use the library's Input component for the search bar and add 'role="button"', 'tabIndex={0}', and 'aria-label' attributes to the grid cells for keyboard support.

issues.description: Grid cells disappear when filtered out, causing the layout to shift dramatically rather than showing empty slots, and there is no visual feedback on the selected element.
issues.fix: Keep the grid layout consistent by applying 'visibility: hidden' or 'opacity: 0' to unmatched elements instead of removing them, and add a 'selected' border or ring to the active cell.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements the complex CSS Grid layout and core logic. However, it misses the required legend and lacks sufficient focus on accessibility and interactive visual feedback, making the UI feel slightly incomplete.

evaluation.critique: The component correctly implements a grid-based periodic table with proper positioning and responsive structure, but has several token usage inconsistencies: the search input uses inline styles with fallback hex values instead of pure design tokens, and padding values use raw numbers instead of spacing tokens. The detail panel lacks visual feedback for interactions, and there's no legend implementation as mentioned in the original prompt requirements.
